messages.author: elperronegro666
messages.text: Building a simple Meshcore network connecting hillside villages of the Alpujarras, Granada, Spain. Construyendo una red de malla simple que conecta las aldeas de la ladera de Alpujarras, Granada, España. https://nomadmesh.org
messages.content_html: Building a simple Meshcore network connecting hillside villages of the Alpujarras, Granada, Spain. Construyendo una red de malla simple que conecta las aldeas de la ladera de Alpujarras, Granada, España. https://nomadmesh.org
embeds_html:
NomadNet · Alpujarras Community Meshcore Network
Meshcore: a community-owned mesh network in the Alpujarras, Spain. Reliable local connectivity, resilient by design.

messages.author: elperronegro666
messages.text: The switch over to EU Narrow has produced excellent improvements to the reliability, speed and distance of message sending/receiving. Now standard settings here
messages.content_html: The switch over to EU Narrow has produced excellent improvements to the reliability, speed and distance of message sending/receiving. Now standard settings here

messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.text: Hello there! Good to see that someone is working on meshcore in ES! I am doing some initial tests around Madrid. Looks prommising, but we need a lot of nodes here
messages.content_html: Hello there! Good to see that someone is working on meshcore in ES! I am doing some initial tests around Madrid. Looks prommising, but we need a lot of nodes here

messages.author: elperronegro666
messages.text: Hi there! Yes, its a slow start. A lot of talking to myself or a few friends. But, from small shoots, large trees can grow. Good luck with your project
messages.content_html: Hi there! Yes, its a slow start. A lot of talking to myself or a few friends. But, from small shoots, large trees can grow. Good luck with your project

messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.text: Hi. Why narrow? I am doing tests now on standard settings. Will narrow be better? In terms of range? It might be crucial in our situation, as we live on desert Did some long range test recently. And in moderate conditions ive managed to send/recive from about 6km. Base with 20cm antena vs t1000e.
messages.content_html: Hi. Why narrow? I am doing tests now on standard settings. Will narrow be better? In terms of range? It might be crucial in our situation, as we live on desert Did some long range test recently. And in moderate conditions ive managed to send/recive from about 6km. Base with 20cm antena vs t1000e.

messages.author: elperronegro666
messages.text: People are generally finding narrow to be more reliable and extending ranges. Less interference too. I achieved 12km LOS before the change should be more now but mountain in the way until i extend the network in the next 3 weeks. Where are you based?
messages.content_html: People are generally finding narrow to be more reliable and extending ranges. Less interference too. I achieved 12km LOS before the change should be more now but mountain in the way until i extend the network in the next 3 weeks. Where are you based?

messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.text: Northern suburbs of Madrid. So close to the mountains. We could try to arrange "long range tests", but let me get a yagi first
messages.content_html: Northern suburbs of Madrid. So close to the mountains. We could try to arrange "long range tests", but let me get a yagi first
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.text: We have to go higg in the mountains or get the baloons
messages.content_html: We have to go higg in the mountains or get the baloons

messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.text: Well, not a chance. Sierra de Andújar between us.
messages.content_html: Well, not a chance. Sierra de Andújar between us.

messages.author: elperronegro666
messages.text: Yeah there's no chance until the Meshcore network infrastructure builds here in Spain
messages.content_html: Yeah there's no chance until the Meshcore network infrastructure builds here in Spain

messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.content_html: Baloon is the answer

messages.author: BobCat
messages.text: Pasaros por el grupo de Telegram de España de la comunidad Meshcore, https://t.me/mcoreXAT
messages.content_html: Pasaros por el grupo de Telegram de España de la comunidad Meshcore, https://t.me/mcoreXAT
embeds_html:
MESHCORE SPAIN
Grupo dedicado a promocionar el uso de Meshcore en España
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: elperronegro666
messages.text: NomadMesh - we now have a Solar Repeater set up in Orgiva which expands our network enormously. Feel free to join in. #MeshCore is a multi platform system for enabling secure text based communications utilizing #LoRa radio hardware. It can be used for Off-Grid Communication, Emergency Response & Disaster Recovery, Outdoor Activities and also IoT sensor networks. More details at https://nomadmesh.org/ NomadMesh: ahora tenemos un repetidor solar instalado en Orgiva que amplía enormemente nuestra red. Siéntete libre de unirte. MeshCore es un sistema multiplataforma que permite comunicaciones seguras basadas en texto utilizando hardware de radio LoRa. Se puede utilizar para comunicación fuera de la red, respuesta a emergencias y recuperación ante desastres, actividades al aire libre y también redes de sensores de IoT. Más detalles en https://nomadmesh.org/
